Ladies and gentlemen, welcome to Profit and Prosper. This is a new episode. We're going to go over efficiencies. So I'm going through all my day to day and I go to masterminds, I go to events, I talk to high level business people every day. And recently I had somebody give me one of the most tangible, just actionable pieces of data I've ever gotten. It was a true piece of wisdom that I've applied in every area of my life and I want to give it to you guys and it relates to efficiencies. So efficiencies by definition are in my terms something that you can do that's solution based to your life, to any part of your life that condenses the timeline and increases the impact of result. So an engineer thinks with this mindset, they think with what is the smallest amount of input they can put into something, what's the largest amount of output that they can get from it. It's an efficiency. So in your life you come across things every single day that may hold you back or stump you or be a wall if you will, whatever they may be. It could be in any form once again in any area of your life. In my day to day I have different categories. I want to break them down for you guys here. So obviously I have a big focus on business. I was running a 19 person digital agency that did 500K last year. I have a lot of partnerships, clients, I have people that I have to deliver for every day. Business is a big category. But when it comes to personal, a lot of what I'm doing here is breaking down things like diet, time and relationships, friends going to this category, best friends going to this category. In this personal section, this is everything that relates once again to your personal life. Now also create efficiencies and material items. I love to break this down also for my goals here. This is a lot of what I do. Whatever I set goals, I break it down into these three sections. So I just constantly think of this trifecta mindset if you will. When it comes to how I break down different areas of my life and how I start listing things out for where I need to become efficient. So let me give you an example. Running a 19 person staff, it's tough. If you guys are a business owner of any scale that has any team, you guys can relate. You have to be a leader at all times. You're constantly an example. You can never have a shot, a shoulder to cry on if you will. You don't have a position inside of your organization and the people that you lead and the people externally that you lead to have a moment where you look like you may be weak. You have to understand that your team is constantly looking for you to help better them. So you always have to think with this efficiency. How can you look at your current unit? How can you currently look at your pack of wolves that roll with you and say, what could they be doing in their day to day that can make them more efficient, more productive? What could get them a bigger result? What could enable them to take less action? I constantly in my team, I'm buying tools, resources, education products. I'm trying to do things that put people in a state where they can take less action to get more result. That's how I've been able to grow as an executive. I've taken the larger cycles that I focused on. I've been able to efficiently handle them throughout the day. People ask me all the time, you're constantly stumped. Like Jeremy, how do you do the amount of shit you do in a day? The answer is simple. I constantly look for ways that I can take simpler actions to give me higher impact results. Just for me telling myself that, I'm in a state where I've now conditioned myself to see those opportunities. There's not a lot of hard work that goes into this mindset. It's a lot of conditioning you have to undo. You're probably like I was back in the day before I had this mindset, before I got this great piece of advice from this mentor. You're in a state, realistically, where you think that things may be hard if you ever tell yourself that before you do something it's tough. Or like if you're in the middle of a workout and before you do those last reps and you say to yourself you can't do it, you're never going to have the opportunity to create efficiencies because you're not looking for what you can simplify in the actions you're taking to get a larger result. Another example, it's though it into personal and you're working out. I just got a personal trainer. My girlfriend, Queen Jacqueline, put me in a state where I on a three times a week, I'm going to this personal trainer. I used to work out five times a week. I used to think about how I knew how to work out. I used to think I had an idea of how to diet. I used to just think I knew all this stuff, right? Until I paid a guy $150 a session. A personal trainer, myself and my girlfriend. Boy was I wrong about everything I thought I knew. I thought that I was being efficient when I worked out. Did you know that when you do shoulders, if you're doing your friends, like I was, like this, you should actually be sitting on a bench and you should be letting your shoulders dangle and you should actually be putting your arms out a little more than you normally do and then going up at a slight angle because it actually targets the peak of your shoulder rather than when you're doing this type of workout, it just targets the front of your shoulder and you don't get any definition where it matters most to actually get the pop. There's so many little things that you can do when you're working out. For example, when I was doing chest, I never felt literally my entire chest from left to right, top to bottom, be as swell as it was after I did simpler workouts with different hand angles or positioning my arms differently or positioning the angles I was at or I didn't know. All the little things I learned from supplements, diets, cheats that you can use, like the everlasting weight belt that you can tie on. I'm not talking like when you do squats and deadlifts, I'm talking about the elastic belt that you put around your waist that burns additional fat here while you're just working out doing what you're normally doing. There's so many little things, once again, that create efficiencies in your day to day. They just enable you. They're the simple little things. Think about it. Everything I just said, it's so simple to tie an elastic band around your waist and the result you get from it is immense, comparatively, to not having it. It's a simple action. You tweaking your diet, simple action to get much bigger results. Since also my girlfriend, Jacqueline, put me on a new diet plan, I've completely positioned myself differently with my dated energy, the consistency of my energy. The way that I operate, my eye color has changed, my physical composition has changed, my ability to get results quicker, a.k.a. creating efficiencies through diet long-term, position my body for more efficiency that I would have had if I didn't tweak that one simple thing that gave me a wide variety of results. The last thing I want to add to this mindset of efficiencies is what's called a multi-purposed action. It helps you comprehend the simplicity behind this mentality. It's so simple. All you have to do is think, what is one action I can take that gets me multiple results at one time? I'm going to say it again. It's so simple. What is, where you currently are up here at Point A? What's the one action you can take that instead of just getting you one result, what's the one action that you can take that gets you a wide variety of results? All of them are at Point B to you. Have you ever thought about having a B.1, Point 2, Point 3, Point 4, and Point 5 just from 1.8 that you started off at? Have you ever thought about how many more things you can get done if you just open up your mind to the fact that you can accomplish more than one thing with one action? It's very simple, guys. All you have to do to have a mindset where you're constantly looking for what you can simplify in your life to get a larger result and what you can do that can get you more than one. All you have to do to have a mindset, simple mindset, is going to help you prop in the process. I'll see you guys in the next video.
